summary: PR 2518 links `src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.c` by moving the object from NonMatching to Matching, completing `grZebesRoute_8020B548` according to the bot report, and adjusting related symbol metadata for grZe route literals/data.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source update; removes nonmatching marker for `grZebesRoute_8020B548`, rewrites light-object loop/control flow, uses HSD_ASSERT, inlines literals/colors, and removes named literal data definitions.
Build configuration update marking `melee/gr/grzebesroute.c` as Matching instead of NonMatching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py Object(NonMatching) -> Object(Matching) for melee/gr/grzebesroute.c

key_files.role: Symbol metadata update converting several grZe route string/float/color symbols to local labels and adding local section labels around grZe route data.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt grZe_Route_* entries changed to @187, @270, @274, @211-@278 and local labels

what_changed: `grZebesRoute_8020B548` no longer has a nonmatching marker and is reported by the bot as a new 100.00% match.
Several file-name, assert-string, float, and color constants were removed as named C objects and replaced by `__FILE__`, `HSD_ASSERT`, inline float literals, and inline `GXColor` initializers.
The light-object traversal in `grZebesRoute_8020B548` was expressed as a bounded `for` loop over up to three `HSD_LObj` nodes, with `HSD_LObjGetNext` in the increment expression.
Small inline helper functions were added around `light_gobj->hsd_obj`, `HSD_LObjGetNext`, and `lobj->flags & LOBJ_TYPE_MASK` in `grZebesRoute_8020B548`.
A temporary padding array in the lower-point-light branch was removed.
`symbols.txt` was updated to represent several grZe route literals as local labels rather than global `grZe_Route_*` symbols.

smart_moves.text: The PR focused the functional source work on the remaining `grZebesRoute_8020B548` match and then linked the entire object only after the bot reported that function as 100.00%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot `1 new match` for `grZebesRoute_8020B548`

smart_moves.text: The source avoids preserving address-named literals for ordinary constants and assert strings, using inline literals and standard assert/report forms instead.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a focused matching PR, ordinary literals and inline aggregate initializers can be preferable to address-named C globals when the symbol metadata is adjusted to local labels and the text match is preserved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: named f32/GXColor globals removed from grzebesroute.c
raw_diff: corresponding symbols.txt entries changed from global grZe_Route_* names to local @ labels
text_corpus: bot reports linked data and one new matched function

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded assert blocks in stage code can be cleaned back to `HSD_ASSERT` when the assertion line and expression are preserved.
A natural bounded loop over object links can replace manual `while` counter/update structure when it matches the intended traversal shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `while (i < 3 && lobj != NULL)` with manual increments changed to `for (i = 0; i < 3 && lobj != NULL; i++, lobj = ...)`

naming_conventions.convention: Use role names like `gobj`, `first_lobj`, and `lobj` for local HSD object pointers when the role is clear.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local `light_gobj` renamed to `gobj`; existing `first_lobj` and `lobj` roles retained

naming_conventions.convention: Do not keep global `grZe_Route_*` names for ordinary literals when metadata and source cleanup support local labels or inline literals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt `grZe_Route_804DB918` etc. changed to local `@211` etc.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use small file-local inline wrappers for accesses/calls (`hsd_obj`, `HSD_LObjGetNext`, and `LOJB_TYPE_MASK` flag extraction) when needed by the matched function shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR evidence shows these wrappers in the matching diff, but contains no author comment explaining why each wrapper was required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace generated-looking named constant loads with inline float and `GXColor` literals while moving corresponding symbol entries to local labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: source inline literals and color initializers

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Remove padding locals once the surrounding code shape no longer requires them.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: deleted `int pad[3];` from lower point-light branch
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: No separate objdiff explanation is present beyond the bot's final match report.

follow_up_queries: stage route GXColor inline initializer matching

notes: The diff and bot report strongly support the changed files, matching outcome for `grZebesRoute_8020B548`, and object link change.
The PR body is empty and there are no inline review comments, so author intent and reviewer rationale are not available.
No local build log, objdiff output, or manual validation transcript is present beyond the decomp-dev bot summary.
Inline helper functions appear to be matching tactics, but the slice does not prove why each helper was necessary.

accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2518 completed `grZebesRoute_8020B548` and linked `melee/gr/grzebesroute.c` by rewriting generated-looking assert/literal data into `HSD_ASSERT`, `__FILE__`, inline floats/colors, a bounded `for` traversal over `HSD_LObj`, and adjusted local symbol labels.

source_update_candidates.title: grZe route literals converted to local labels
source_update_candidates.text: Several `grZe_Route_*` data/sdata2 symbols for grzebesroute literals were changed to local labels such as `@187`, `@270`, `@274`, and `@211`-`@278`, while source uses inline literals and colors.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible data/symbol ownership note; should not be generalized without checking current symbol metadata and neighboring route TUs.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a broad rule that all route constants should be inlined; this PR only shows grzebesroute-specific literal and symbol changes.
Do not record reviewer rationale beyond approval; the loaded review body is empty.
Do not treat the inline wrapper tactic as a global standard; the PR lacks explanation or independent objdiff evidence for each wrapper.
